framework: Schelling focal point detection + physical observables
design: 两层观测: 物理层(状态)+ 焦点层(转折). History in geopulse.db

history.est: 1-2 declining, 15-point plan delivered
history.note: Day 26. CENTCOM: 9000+ targets struck, 140+ vessels destroyed. Cooper: fire declining to 1-2 at a time. ISW: 85% SAMs destroyed. BUT: (1) Iranian missile struck JERUSALEM Tue AM (AFP); (2) Tel Aviv hit - 6 wounded (ToI); (3) CENTCOM video Mar 23: Arash-2 + Shahed-136 destroyed; (4) 82nd Airborne deploying 3000 soldiers (WSJ); (5) US dead: 14 confirmed (AJE/Time); (6) ★★★★★ 15-POINT PEACE PLAN delivered to Iran via Pakistan Army Chief Asim Munir (NYT Mar 24). Trump: 'in negotiations right now'. Iran publicly denies. JD Vance may attend Islamabad talks next week (Guardian). PATTERN: Volume collapse continues. 82nd Airborne deployment = ground escalation option. But 15-point plan + Pakistan mediation = FIRST STRUCTURED EXIT PATH. Source: CENTCOM/NYT/WSJ/CNBC/Guardian/AFP/ToI/AJE

history.est: 1-2 declining, coordinated offensive announced
history.note: Day 26. ★★★★ IRGC announced COORDINATED OFFENSIVE by IRGC Navy + Army + Hezbollah against Israel + US bases (Wikipedia/AJE). 58 hospitalized in Israel from missile strike. Iran targeted Gulf countries with missiles+drones AGAIN (oil prices rose). BUT CENTCOM: fire still 1-2 at a time, volume collapsed. 9000+ targets struck, 140+ vessels destroyed. US dead: 14 confirmed (13 combat + 1 health) + 6 refueling aircraft crash. AJE: Pakistan shared US ceasefire demands with Iran, awaiting response. 82nd Airborne 1000+ additional soldiers approved by Trump (NBC). PATTERN: IRGC attempting to demonstrate capability via 'coordinated offensive' label but physical volume remains degraded. Scope expanding (Gulf states hit again, 58 hospitalized Israel) but mass salvo era over. Source: AJE/NBC/CBS/Wikipedia/AP

history.est: 1-2 declining, IRGC 'precision-guided' salvos at Israel+Gulf+US bases
history.note: Day 26 Wed. ★★★★ IRAN COUNTERPROPOSAL + CONTINUED STRIKES: (1) IRGC fired 'precision-guided' missiles+drones at Israel + US bases in Kuwait/Jordan/Bahrain Wed AM (Euronews/AP); (2) Drones hit fuel tank at Kuwait International Airport — fireball (Euronews); (3) Shrapnel fell near Amman, Jordan; air raid warnings in Bahrain; (4) IDF continued wide-scale strikes on Tehran infrastructure Wed; (5) 82nd Airborne 2000-3000 deploying (NPR/WSJ confirmed written orders); (6) CENTCOM: 9000+ targets, 140+ vessels; ISW: 85% SAMs destroyed; (7) Iran death toll: 3230+ (HRANA); US: 14 combat + 1 health + 6 crash = 21 total; (8) IRGC volume STILL declining (1-2 at a time) but hitting WIDER targets — Kuwait airport is NEW. PATTERN: Quantitative degradation confirmed but GEOGRAPHIC expansion (Kuwait airport = civilian infrastructure). Source: Euronews/AP/NPR/WSJ/CENTCOM/ISW/HRANA

history.est: 1-2 declining, IRGC 'coordinated offensive' label but volume still collapsed
history.note: Day 27 Thu. CENTCOM: 9000+ targets struck, 140+ vessels destroyed. Cooper: fire declining to 1-2 at a time (confirmed). ISW: 85% SAMs destroyed. IRGC announced 'coordinated offensive' (IRGC Navy+Army+Hezbollah) but PHYSICAL volume remains 1-2 at a time — label vs reality. IRGC fired 'precision-guided' missiles+drones at Israel+Kuwait Int'l Airport (fireball on fuel tank, Euronews)+Jordan (shrapnel near Amman)+Bahrain+US bases. IDF continued wide-scale wave on Tehran (Mehrabad airport, IRGC base, police station struck). 82nd Airborne 2-3K deploying (NYT/CNN/Politico confirmed). Trump: Iran has 4 MORE DAYS before energy infrastructure targeted = ULTIMATUM RESET from Mar 23 postponement. US dead: 21 total. Iran dead: 3230+ (HRANA). PATTERN: Volume collapse confirmed by CENTCOM but SCOPE still expanding — Kuwait Int'l Airport is civilian infrastructure escalation. 82nd Airborne = ground option preparation. Source: CENTCOM/ISW/CNN/NYT/Politico/Euronews/AP/HRANA/Wikipedia

history.est: 1-2 declining, CENTCOM 600+ ballistic sites struck
history.note: Day 28. CENTCOM Mar 23 video: Arash-2 + Shahed-136 + mobile launch destroyed. ISW Mar 24: IDF 600+ strikes on ballistic missile sites. Iran fired 9 waves at Israel (cluster munitions continuing). IRGC volume STILL 1-2 at a time — mass salvo capability GONE. Combined force struck Abu Musa Island ports/hangars degrading Iran maritime ops. US dead: 21 total. Iran dead: ~3500+ (estimates). PATTERN: Quantitative degradation confirmed, Iran demonstrating residual capability but mass strike era over. Source: CENTCOM/ISW/AP/AJE

history.est: 1-2 declining, CENTCOM 9000+ targets, IDF 600+ ballistic sites struck
history.note: Day 28. CENTCOM: 9000+ targets struck, 140+ vessels destroyed, 85% SAMs gone. IDF: 600+ strikes on ballistic missile sites. Combined force struck Abu Musa Island ports/hangars. Iran still firing 1-2 at a time (Jerusalem/Tel Aviv/Kuwait airport). US dead: 21 total (14 KIA + 6 crash + 1 health). 290+ wounded. Senate REJECTED War Powers 47-53 (Rand Paul lone R yes). 82nd Airborne 2-3K deploying (written orders). PATTERN: Volume collapse confirmed, IRGC demonstrating residual capability. Source: CENTCOM/ISW/AJE/Wikipedia/Time/Guardian/Politico

history.est: 1-2 declining, 13 US bases uninhabitable, USS Lincoln ops continue
history.note: Day 28. ★★★★ NEW: NYT/DefSecAsia: 13 US bases in ME nearly UNINHABITABLE from IRGC sustained strikes — troops dispersed to hotels/offices (NYT Mar 25). CENTCOM: 9000+ targets struck, 140+ vessels destroyed. USS Abraham Lincoln continues flight ops against Iran targets (CENTCOM Mar 26). IDF: 600+ ballistic sites struck. IRGC fire STILL 1-2 at a time (mass salvo era over). BUT: (1) IRGC 'precision-guided' missiles+drones at Israel+Kuwait airport+Jordan+Bahrain (Euronews/AP); (2) 82nd Airborne 2000 soldiers deploying with written orders (NYT/CNN/USAToday); (3) Senate REJECTED War Powers 47-53; (4) Iran fired 9 waves at Israel (cluster munitions continuing); (5) US dead: 21 total, 290+ wounded; (6) Iran dead: ~3500+. PATTERN: IRGC volume collapsed but BASE DEGRADATION is real operational cost — 13 bases uninhabitable = most significant infrastructure damage since Desert Storm. Source: NYT/DefSecAsia/CENTCOM/CNN/USAToday/AP

history.est: 1-2 declining, CENTCOM destroyed 2/3 arms manufacturing
history.note: Day 28 21:00. CENTCOM chief: destroyed 2/3 of Iran arms manufacturing (ToI Mar 25). CENTCOM struck 9th Artesh Air Force Tactical Airbase Bandar Abbas Mar 25 (ISW). AJE live tracker: 1,937 killed + 24,800 injured Iran total. 6 US crew killed in refueling crash Iraq Mar 13. Fire STILL 1-2 at a time (Cooper confirmed). ISW: 85% SAMs destroyed. Combined force struck Abu Musa Island ports. US dead: 14 confirmed KIA (Wikipedia) + 6 crash + 1 health = 21. PATTERN: IRGC volume collapse accelerating — arms manufacturing destruction removes regeneration capacity. Residual precision strikes (Jerusalem/Tel Aviv/Gulf) = demonstrating political will not military capability. Source: ToI/ISW/AJE/CENTCOM/Wikipedia

history.est: ~1.5 mbd (2-3 ships/day, Iran IMO offer)
history.note: Day 26. (1) ★★★ Iran told IMO 'non-hostile vessels' may transit if they coordinate with Iranian authorities (Reuters Mar 24) — FIRST FORMAL TRANSIT FRAMEWORK; (2) ★★★ Bahrain proposed UNSC resolution authorizing 'all necessary means' to protect Hormuz shipping; France tabled rival text (Reuters Mar 24); (3) Zombie ships with AIS off transiting (ABC AU); (4) Windward: 2.3/day visible, declining; (5) Kalshi: <25% odds of normal transit before Apr 15, 67% by Jun 1; (6) Trump 5-day pause still active (2 days remain, deadline ~Mar 28); (7) CNBC: tanker traffic won't return for months. PATTERN: Iran formalizing transit regime at IMO = institutional path to partial reopening. Bahrain UNSC draft = military enforcement path. Two competing frameworks emerging. Source: Reuters/CNBC/ABC AU/Windward/Kalshi/IMO

history.est: ~1.5 mbd (2-3 ships/day, IMO framework + UNSC drafts)
history.note: Day 26. (1) Iran told IMO 'non-hostile vessels' may transit if they coordinate with Iranian authorities (Reuters Mar 24) — ships with no ties to Israel/US allowed (NYT); (2) Bahrain proposed UNSC resolution 'all necessary means' for Hormuz; France tabled rival text (Reuters); (3) Windward: 2.3/day visible, zombie ships with AIS off also transiting; (4) Brent CRASHED below $100 on ceasefire hopes — $98.28 (-6%) Wed Asian; (5) Kalshi: <25% normal transit before Apr 15, 67% by Jun 1; (6) Trump 5-day pause still active (1 day remaining, deadline ~Mar 28); (7) CNBC: tanker traffic won't return for months. PATTERN: TWO competing transit frameworks emerging — Iran's unilateral IMO regime vs Bahrain's UNSC military enforcement. Oil price crash suggests market pricing in diplomatic resolution despite zero physical change in transit volume. Source: Reuters/NYT/CNBC/Windward/Kalshi

history.est: ~1.5 mbd (2-3 ships/day, IRAN COUNTERPROPOSAL CLAIMS HORMUZ SOVEREIGNTY)
history.note: Day 26 Wed. ★★★★★ IRAN'S 5-CONDITION COUNTERPROPOSAL CLAIMS HORMUZ SOVEREIGNTY: (1) Iran rejected US 15-point plan (AP/NPR/NYT Mar 25); (2) Iran issued OWN counterproposal via Press TV: demands reparations + sovereignty over Strait of Hormuz (NPR); (3) Iran told IMO 'non-hostile vessels' may transit if coordinated with Iran (Reuters Mar 24); (4) CNBC: Brent -3.8% to $100.54, WTI -3.6% to $89.01 on talks hopes despite Iran rejection; (5) Goldman base case: Hormuz normalize in April over 4 weeks; (6) Windward: 2.3/day visible + zombie ships AIS-off; (7) Bahrain UNSC draft 'all necessary means' + France rival text — vote pending; (8) Trump: Iran gave him 'very big present' related to Hormuz. PATTERN: TWO frameworks now THREE — Iran unilateral IMO regime + Bahrain UNSC military + Iran counterproposal claiming sovereignty. Source: AP/NPR/NYT/Reuters/CNBC/Euronews/Goldman

history.est: ~1.5-2 mbd (5 ships/day Mon — SLIGHT UPTICK from 2.3)
history.note: Day 27. ★★★ AJE/Windward: 5 vessels tracked via AIS on Monday Mar 24 (UP from 2.3/day avg). BBC: ~100 ships since Mar 1. Iran told IMO 'non-hostile vessels' may transit if coordinated with Iranian authorities (Reuters Mar 24). Bahrain UNSC resolution 'all necessary means' facing opposition — Russia/China likely to VETO (Reuters). France tabled rival text. UN chief Guterres: war 'out of control'. Iran IMO framework = institutional path to partial reopening. BUT no physical change in mine threat, insurance barriers persist. Trump: 4 MORE DAYS → if power plants struck, IRGC complete closure threat reactivates. Goldman base case: Hormuz normalize in April over 4 weeks. Kalshi: <25% normal transit before Apr 15, 67% by Jun 1. Source: AJE/Windward/BBC/Reuters/Fox/UN News/Goldman/Kalshi

history.est: ~2.5 mbd (5-6 ships/day — UPTICK from 2.3)
history.note: Day 28. ★★★ TRANSIT UPTICK: Kpler (India Today Mar 25): 138 ships crossed in March including 87 oil/gas tankers = 5-6/day (UP from 2.3/day Windward). 95% drop from pre-crisis but improving. Lloyd's List: 20+ vessels took 'Tehran-approved route' through Iranian territorial waters via Larak Island. Iran charging ~$2M fee for safe passage. NEW: Iran appointed hardliner Zolghadr as SNSC secretary (ISW). Maham 3/7 mines confirmed (12+); CENTCOM destroyed 44 minelaying vessels. Abu Musa Island ports struck degrading IRGC maritime coordination. White House 'tracking closely' how to get tankers through (US News). PATTERN: Transit IMPROVING despite rejection. Iran monetizing passage. Source: Kpler/India Today/Lloyd's List/ISW/US News

history.est: ~2.5 mbd (5-6 ships/day — UPTICK CONFIRMED by Kpler)
history.note: Day 28. ★★★ TRANSIT UPTICK CONFIRMED: Kpler (India Today): 138 ships March = 5-6/day (UP from 2.3/day). 87 oil/gas tankers. Still 95% drop from pre-crisis. Lloyd's List: 20+ vessels took Tehran-approved Larak Island route. Iran charging ~M/ship. Iran told IMO non-hostile vessels may transit. Iran REJECTED 15-point plan + counterproposed: Hormuz sovereignty + reparations + Lebanon in ceasefire. UNSC: Bahrain draft vs France text, Russia/China likely VETO. Goldman base case: normalize April. Source: Kpler/India Today/Lloyd's List/NYT/CNBC/Guardian

history.est: ~2.5-3 mbd (5-6 ships/day, CHINA TRANSIT CONFIRMED)
history.note: Day 28. ★★★★ CHINA TRANSIT CONFIRMED: (1) CSMonitor Mar 25: Chinese-owned 'Newvoyager' container ship sailed OUT of Hormuz skirting mines — FIRST confirmed Chinese vessel; (2) SCMP Mar 25: Chinese-owned 'Lucky Gas' transited via Larak Island route to Oman's Sohar; (3) Iran FM Araghchi: 5 'friendly nations' (China/India/Russia/Pakistan/Iraq) get OPEN passage; US/Israel/Gulf allies BLOCKED (India Today Mar 26); (4) Kpler: 138 ships March = 5-6/day, 87 oil/gas tankers; (5) Lloyd's List: 20+ vessels took Tehran-approved Larak Island route, ~$2M fee each; (6) ISW: IRGC verifies vessel details at Larak Island checkpoint; (7) Araghchi: 'We are in a state of war...it remains open to others'; (8) NYT: Iran told IMO 'non-hostile' ships may transit. PATTERN: Iran FORMALIZING two-tier transit regime — allies get passage, enemies blocked. China becoming ANCHOR CUSTOMER of Iran's new Hormuz order. Source: CSMonitor/SCMP/India Today/Kpler/Lloyd's List/ISW/NYT/Reuters

history.est: ~2.5-3 mbd (5-6 ships/day, two-tier regime solidifying)
history.note: Day 28 21:00. BBC: ~100 ships since Mar 1. Windward Mar 22: 16 AIS-visible in 7 days = near collapse, BUT Kpler: 138 ships in March = 5-6/day (improvement). Iran told UN/IMO: 'non-hostile' vessels may transit if coordinated (Reuters Mar 24). Lloyd's List: 20+ vessels via Larak Island route, ~$2M fee each. Saudi/UAE bypassing via pipelines (East-West to Yanbu, ADCOP to Fujairah). India/Pakistan sent destroyers to Gulf of Oman escort. TWO-TIER regime solidifying: 5 friendly nations (CN/IN/RU/PK/IQ) get passage, US/Israel/Gulf allies BLOCKED. PATTERN: Physical transit improving slightly (2.3→5-6/day) as Iran monetizes access. Still 95% below pre-crisis (17-20 mbd). Source: BBC/Windward/Kpler/Reuters/Lloyd's List

q3_spr_release_rate.label: SPR实际释放速率(mbd)
q3_spr_release_rate.delivery_timeline: 120 days per DOE, first barrels end of W3

history.est: ~0.75 mbd first batch, SPR NOISE vs diplomacy
history.note: Day 26 D+11. SPR first shipments began Fri Mar 21 (Argus). DOE 45.2M bbl to Shell/Marathon/BP/Gunvor. Apr-May delivery = ~0.75 mbd. 412M bbl total global coordinated release (32 countries). Mon close: Brent 99.94 (-11%), WTI 88.52 (-9.9%) on Trump talks. Tue rebound: Brent ~101-103 (+4%), WTI ~91-92 (+4%). Market whipsawing on diplomacy signals not supply. Gas: ~.96/gal (+33% from .98 pre-war). Pentagon requesting B supplemental (UPI). CAP: war already cost B in 4 weeks. IEA Birol: crisis worse than 1970s+Ukraine COMBINED. SPR trickle IRRELEVANT vs /bbl daily geopolitical swings. Source: Argus/CNBC/NYT/CAP/UPI/IEA/Fortune

history.est: ~0.75 mbd first batch, DOE 'record speed'
history.note: Day 26 D+11. SPR first shipments began Fri Mar 21 (Argus). DOE: deliveries at 'record speeds' (BIC Magazine). 45.2M bbl first batch to Shell/Marathon/BP/Gunvor, Apr-May delivery = ~0.75 mbd. Exchange: return 55M bbl (22% premium). 172M bbl US total, 400M bbl global coordinated (32 countries). NTU: 172M = 40% of SPR inventory, draws to 34% capacity. OPIS: half will be loaned not sold. Market: Brent $98.28 Wed (-6%), WTI $87.68 (-5.1%) — oil crashing on DIPLOMACY not SPR. SPR trickle irrelevant vs $15/bbl geopolitical swings. Gas: ~$3.98/gal national (+33%). CAP: war cost $25B by end of week. Source: Argus/BIC/DOE/NTU/OPIS/CAP/ToI/CNBC

history.est: ~0.75 mbd first batch, DOE 'record speed'
history.note: Day 26 D+12. SPR first shipments began Fri Mar 21 (Argus). DOE: deliveries at 'record speeds' (BIC). 45.2M bbl first batch to Shell/Marathon/BP/Gunvor (52% of 86M subscribed). MARKET: Brent $100.54 (-3.8%), WTI $89.01 (-3.6%) Wed — oil declining on DIPLOMACY not SPR. SPR trickle irrelevant vs $15/bbl geopolitical swings. Gas ~$3.98/gal national (+33%). Goldman base case: Hormuz normalize in April. Source: Argus/BIC/DOE/NTU/CNBC/Goldman

NPR: 150M+ bbl from TX/LA salt caverns. MARKET TODAY: Brent $104.04 (+rebound from $94.78 diplomacy dip), WTI $91.84. fp1_china_action.label: 中国实质行动(唯一可行聚点注入)
fp1_china_action.findings: Day 28 21:00. ★★★ WANG YI RHETORIC UPGRADE: (1) China FM Wang Yi: 'glimmer of hope' in Iran war diplomacy (ChinaDaily/MilliChronicle Mar 26 TODAY); (2) Wang spoke with Iranian FM + regional counterparts, urged renewed dialogue; (3) Signals from both US and Iran suggesting willingness to re-engage diplomatically; (4) BUT Tehran publicly insists will NOT negotiate (Reuters Mar 26); (5) White House warned 'unleash hell' if Iran doesn't accept peace plan; (6) Wikipedia/JINSA: China providing limited material assistance (missile spare parts) + intel cooperation; (7) Chinese vessels CONTINUE using Larak Island route (Kpler/SCMP confirmed). NOISE FILTER: Wang Yi statement = RHETORIC not ACTION. No envoy deployed, no new mediation initiative, no naval presence change. China remains ANCHOR CUSTOMER of Iran's two-tier Hormuz regime, not MEDIATOR. ASSESSMENT: fp1 still below threshold. Wang Yi rhetoric ≠ Xi Jinping envoy/summit/naval deployment. Source: ChinaDaily/MilliChronicle/Reuters/JINSA/SCMP

fp2_us_signal_inconsistency.label: 美方信号包内部矛盾(承诺解锁前导)
fp2_us_signal_inconsistency.findings: Day 28 21:00. ★★★★★ CONTRADICTION AT PEAK: (1) Trump Mar 3: escort 'as soon as possible' — STILL NOT HAPPENING (FP Mar 24: 'Empty Words'); (2) Trump Mar 23: postponed power plant strikes 5 days citing 'productive talks'; (3) NOW DEADLINE ~Mar 28-29 — 2-3 DAYS remaining; (4) Pentagon: 2,000 82nd Airborne troops deploying (NYT Mar 24); (5) Jalili: Trump shifted from 'escort' to 'insure' to 'manage jointly' = PROGRESSIVE WALK-BACK; (6) Iran DENIES talks entirely (NPR/Reuters Mar 26); (7) Trump today: Iran 'desperate for a deal' (Reuters); (8) Senate REJECTED War Powers 53-47 — Rand Paul LONE R YES (Time Mar 25); (9) 13 bases nearly uninhabitable (NYT Mar 25); (10) USS Lincoln continues flight ops (CENTCOM Mar 26). PATTERN: Military escalation + diplomatic rhetoric + approaching deadline = MAXIMUM TENSION. Either escalation to energy infrastructure or another face-saving extension by Mar 29. Source: FP/NPR/Reuters/NYT/Time/CENTCOM

fp3_face_saving_framework.label: 面子工程原材料出现
fp3_face_saving_framework.findings: Day 28 21:00. ★★★★★ HIGHEST FP3 SIGNAL YET: (1) Iran rejected US 15-point ceasefire plan (Guardian/AP Mar 25); (2) Iran countered with OWN 5-point 'non-negotiable' demands: reparations + Hormuz sovereignty + Lebanon in ceasefire (NPR/Guardian/OANN); (3) Reuters TODAY Mar 26: Iran says 'reviewing' US proposal, no intention of talks, but proposal 'passed to senior authorities'; (4) Trump: Iran 'desperate for a deal' (Reuters Mar 26); (5) Pakistan offering to mediate/host talks; (6) Gulf states: war poses 'existential threat' (ABC AU); (7) UN Human Rights Council condemned Iran; (8) UNSC: Bahrain draft vs France text pending vote; (9) ABC AU: some Iranian figures considering proposal despite public rejection. ASSESSMENT: FORMAL COUNTERPROPOSAL = ENGAGEMENT. Rejection is negotiating posture, not final position. Two structured proposals on table = first real fp3 materials. 40-45% ceasefire framework within 2 weeks (UP from 35-40%). Source: Guardian/AP/Reuters/NPR/OANN/ABC AU

fp4_cost_threshold.label: 成本阈值的物理逼近
fp4_cost_threshold.findings: Day 28 21:00. (1) Senate BLOCKED War Powers 53-47 — political brake REMOVED (Time/NYT Mar 24-25); (2) Rand Paul lone R yes vote; (3) House Dems 'clamping down on defections' for new vote (Axios Mar 24); (4) Alternative resolution: March 30 deadline to wind down (Cuellar); (5) US dead: 14 KIA confirmed + 6 crash + 1 health = 21 total (Wikipedia); (6) Gas up $1 in March: national ~$3.93, CA $5.83 (AAA/ET Mar 25); (7) War cost $25B+ in 4 weeks (CAP); Pentagon requesting $200B supplemental; (8) Trump approval 36% (Reuters/Ipsos); (9) 13 US bases uninhabitable (NYT). BUT: Republican bloc holds, no political mechanism to force stop. Cost accumulating on fiscal/infrastructure/casualty vectors but Congress NOT constraining. ASSESSMENT: fp4 SLOW BURN — costs rising but political threshold NOT breached. Would need major US casualties event or gas > national to trigger political inflection. Source: Time/NYT/Axios/AAA/ET/Wikipedia/CAP/Reuters/Ipsos

noise_filter.rule: 按行为类型分类,不按人物分类
noise: 巴盖伊等发言人声明(言辞)
媒体'消息人士'报道(未验证)
signal: Trump签署行政令/军事命令(行动)
美军实际调兵/部署变化(行动)
IRGC实际攻击模式变化(行动)
中国实际经济/军事动作(行动)
noise_filter.key_distinction: 同一个人的言辞=噪声,行动=信号。Trump说'just for fun'=噪声。Trump命令打Kharg=信号。

pipeline_daily_checklist: 1. 物理层: 更新Q1(袭击频率) + Q2(通行量) + Q3(SPR释放)
2. 焦点层: 扫描fp1(中国动作) + fp2(美方矛盾) + fp3(退出框架) + fp4(成本阈值)
3. 噪声过滤: 新闻分类为action vs rhetoric
4. 结构性验证: 跑structural_verify检查盲区+停滞+链断裂
5. DAG更新: 只有物理层变化或焦点层信号触发才更新概率
